title: teucrium rotundifolium subsp. faurei
intro: Teucrium rotundifolium subsp. faurei, also known as the narrow-leaved germander or narrow-leaved teucrium, is a plant species within the genus Teucrium, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and blue to purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic, due to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Teucrium rotundifolium subsp. faurei include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and aid in the treatment of certain skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. Teucrium rotundifolium subsp. faurei can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
